title: Globalisation
paperName: Cambridge International A Level Economics (P3 & P4)
summary: 9708 A Level — global integration of trade, finance, labour, and culture with winners and losers.
learningObjectives: Evaluate costs including inequality and job displacement.

simpleExplanation.title: A connected world
simpleExplanation.summary: Globalisation spreads trade, investment, and ideas. Gains from specialisation coexist with structural unemployment and regulatory races.
steps: Drivers: technology, trade liberalisation, transport costs ↓.
Losers: unskilled in HICs; weak institutions in some LICs.

sections.content: **Globalisation** (11.6) closes topic 11. Structure essays as **benefits → costs → judgement** with **real examples**.

steps.caption: Globalisation: integrated markets — trade, finance, migration, ideas.
Benefits: specialisation, lower prices, technology spread.
Costs: job losses in declining industries, cultural homogenisation.
Winners and losers — evaluate distributional effects.
steps.embedHint: Skilled vs unskilled labour in HICs.
